annotations.label: Chain complex

anchor.snippet: We start with a chain complex, which is a sequence

annotations.note: ChainComplex is defined as a HomologicalComplex with the down-Nat shape, directly formalizing the article's notion.

annotations.label: Group of cycles

anchor.snippet: th group of cycles

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.cycles K i is the i-th cycles object, formalizing ker d_n in a chain complex.

annotations.label: Group of boundaries

anchor.snippet: th group of boundaries

annotations.note: Mathlib has no standalone HomologicalComplex.boundaries; boundaries appear only implicitly through the image-to-cycles factorization and opcycles in the ShortComplex API.

annotations.label: Homology group of a chain complex

anchor.snippet: th homology group

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.homology K i defines the i-th homology of a homological complex (kernel mod image) categorically.

annotations.label: Homology theory

anchor.snippet: To associate a homology theory to other types of mathematical objects

annotations.note: Mathlib has the general categorical homology-functor machinery but no axiomatic Eilenberg–Steenrod "homology theory" structure.

annotations.label: Homology theory as a functor

anchor.snippet: In the language of category theory , a homology theory is a type of functor

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor is the n-th homology as a functor on the category of homological complexes.

annotations.label: Homology of a topological space

anchor.snippet: Perhaps the most familiar usage of the term homology is for the homology of a topological space

annotations.note: AlgebraicTopology.singularHomologyFunctor C n defines the n-th singular homology functor on TopCat with coefficients in C.

annotations.label: Boundary in a chain complex

anchor.snippet: in the context of chain complexes , a boundary is any element of the image

annotations.note: Boundaries appear only implicitly as image of d via the ShortComplex homology data; there is no standalone HomologicalComplex.boundaries decl.

annotations.label: Cycle in a chain complex

anchor.snippet: In the context of chain complexes, a cycle is any element of the kernel

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.cycles K i directly formalizes the kernel of the differential.

annotations.label: Homology groups of a topological space

anchor.snippet: th homology group

annotations.note: The n-th singular homology of a topological space is defined as singularHomologyFunctor C n applied to coefficients and the space.

annotations.label: Homology of the circle

anchor.snippet: A one-dimensional sphere

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of the singular homology of S^1 was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the 2-sphere

anchor.snippet: A two-dimensional sphere

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of the singular homology of S^2 was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the n-sphere

anchor.snippet: In general for an n -dimensional sphere

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of H_*(S^n) was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the 2-ball

anchor.snippet: A two-dimensional ball

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of the singular homology of a disk was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the torus

anchor.snippet: The torus is defined as a product of two circles

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of H_*(T^2) was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the n-torus

anchor.snippet: If n products of a topological space X is written as

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of H_*(T^n) was found.

annotations.label: Homology of the projective plane

anchor.snippet: For the projective plane P , a simple computation shows

annotations.note: No Mathlib computation of H_*(RP^2) was found.

annotations.label: Torsion in homology

anchor.snippet: This phenomenon is called torsion

annotations.note: Module torsion exists in Mathlib but there is no dedicated 'torsion in homology' notion specific to this article's usage.

annotations.label: Chain complex (general construction)

anchor.snippet: A chain complex is a sequence of abelian groups or modules

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex generalizes the article's construction to any complex shape over a category with zero morphisms.

annotations.label: Boundary of a boundary is zero

anchor.snippet: the composition of any two consecutive boundary operators be trivial

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.d_comp_d states that the composition of any two differentials is zero.

annotations.label: Boundaries and cycles

anchor.snippet: Elements of

annotations.note: Cycles are formalized as HomologicalComplex.cycles; boundaries exist only implicitly via the imageToCycles/opcycles factorization in the ShortComplex API.

annotations.label: n-th homology group

anchor.snippet: called the n th homology group of X

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.homology K n is the n-th homology of a homological complex.

annotations.label: Exact chain complex

anchor.snippet: A chain complex is said to be exact if the image

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.ExactAt K i (and exactness at every degree) formalizes the article's notion of an exact chain complex.

annotations.label: Reduced homology groups

anchor.snippet: The reduced homology groups of a chain complex

annotations.note: Loogle finds no decl with reducedHomology in its name.

annotations.label: Simplicial homology groups

anchor.snippet: The simplicial homology groups H n ( X ) of a simplicial complex X are defined

annotations.note: The alternating face map complex of a simplicial object exists and its homology gives simplicial homology, but no top-level `simplicialHomology` decl was found.

annotations.label: Singular homology groups

anchor.snippet: The singular homology groups H n ( X ) are defined for any topological space X

annotations.note: AlgebraicTopology.singularHomologyFunctor is the n-th singular homology functor on TopCat.

annotations.label: Cohomology groups

anchor.snippet: Cohomology groups are formally similar to homology groups

annotations.note: CochainComplex is a HomologicalComplex with up-Nat shape; cohomology is obtained by applying HomologicalComplex.homology to a cochain complex.

annotations.label: Homotopy group

anchor.snippet: The nth homotopy group

annotations.note: Mathlib defines FundamentalGroup (π_1) and has Mathlib/Topology/Homotopy/HomotopyGroup.lean, but a general π_n decl does not appear in the public API surface.

annotations.label: Hurewicz theorem

anchor.snippet: the Hurewicz theorem describes a homomorphism

annotations.note: Loogle finds no Hurewicz-named decl in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hurewicz isomorphism: H_1 is abelianization of pi_1

anchor.snippet: the Hurewicz homomorphism coincides with abelianization

annotations.note: No decl relating H_1 to the abelianization of the fundamental group was found.

annotations.label: Figure eight homotopy vs homology

anchor.snippet: example, suppose

annotations.note: No Mathlib treatment of the figure-eight wedge S^1 ∨ S^1 with its homotopy/homology contrast was found.

annotations.label: Simplicial homology construction

anchor.snippet: The motivating example comes from algebraic topology

annotations.note: The alternating face map complex is the standard model for simplicial homology in Mathlib, but no top-level simplicialHomology functor is exposed.

annotations.label: Singular homology construction

anchor.snippet: Using simplicial homology example as a model, one can define a singular homology

annotations.note: Singular homology is defined as a functor on TopCat via the singular chain complex.

annotations.label: Group homology / derived functors

anchor.snippet: In abstract algebra , one uses homology to define derived functors

annotations.note: groupHomology A n is the n-th group homology of a k-linear G-representation, computed via the inhomogeneous chains complex.

annotations.label: Morphism of chain complexes

anchor.snippet: Chain complexes form a category

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.Hom defines morphisms (chain maps) between homological complexes, making them into a category.

annotations.label: Homology as covariant functor of objects

anchor.snippet: If the chain complex depends on the object X in a covariant manner

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.homologyFunctor C c n is the covariant n-th homology functor on the category of homological complexes.

annotations.label: Cohomology as contravariant functor

anchor.snippet: The only difference between homology and cohomology

annotations.note: CochainComplex provides the cochain framework but the article's specific covariant/contravariant distinction is not packaged as a single decl.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic

anchor.snippet: then we can define the Euler characteristic

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.eulerChar is the Euler characteristic as an alternating sum of ranks of complex objects.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic from homology

anchor.snippet: It turns out that the Euler characteristic can also be computed on the level of homology

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.homologyEulerChar computes the Euler characteristic from homology, matching the chain-level version.

annotations.label: Long exact sequence from short exact sequence

anchor.snippet: Every short exact sequence

annotations.note: HomologicalComplex.HomologySequence.snakeInput packages a short exact sequence of complexes into a SnakeInput, yielding the long exact homology sequence via the snake lemma.

annotations.label: Zig-zag lemma (connecting homomorphisms)

anchor.snippet: The latter are called connecting homomorphisms and are provided by the zig-zag lemma

annotations.note: The connecting homomorphism δ is defined by the snake lemma for SnakeInput, providing the zig-zag/connecting map of the long exact sequence.

annotations.label: Brouwer fixed point theorem

anchor.snippet: The Brouwer fixed point theorem

annotations.note: No Brouwer fixed-point theorem for the disk was found in Mathlib (only unrelated Brouwer-style results in order theory).

annotations.label: Invariance of domain

anchor.snippet: Invariance of domain

annotations.note: Loogle finds no invarianceOfDomain decl.

annotations.label: Hairy ball theorem

anchor.snippet: The Hairy ball theorem

annotations.note: No hairy-ball theorem was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Borsuk–Ulam theorem

anchor.snippet: The Borsuk–Ulam theorem

annotations.note: No Borsuk–Ulam decl was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Invariance of dimension

anchor.snippet: Invariance of dimension

annotations.note: No invariance-of-dimension result was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jordan curve theorem (shrinking curves on sphere)

anchor.snippet: The Jordan curve theorem shows that any closed curve

annotations.note: No Jordan curve theorem was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: is the Klein bottle , which is a torus with a twist

annotations.note: No Klein bottle topological space appears in Mathlib (only Klein four-group, an unrelated namesake).

annotations.label: Projective plane surface

anchor.snippet: The projective plane

annotations.note: Real projective plane as a topological surface does not appear to be formalized.

annotations.label: Closed surfaces from polygon gluing

anchor.snippet: all closed surfaces can be produced by gluing the sides of some polygon

annotations.note: Polygon-gluing construction of closed surfaces is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification via connected sum

anchor.snippet: Any closed 2-manifold can be realised as the connected sum

annotations.note: The classification of closed surfaces via connected sum is not formalized in Mathlib.
